title: acer monspessulanum subsp. oksalianum
intro: Acer monspessulanum subsp. oksalianum, also known as the Montpellier maple or Oksalian maple, is a subspecies of the European field maple (Acer campestre), a deciduous tree native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Acer monspessulanum subsp. oksalianum include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce pain and prevent infections. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other remedies for various health conditions.
